page: Microsoft Azure E8-2s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance asymmetry between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. However, multi-core performance is limited due to the 2-vCPU constraint, resulting in poor ratings for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with uncached read and write speeds categorized as poor, though PassMark memory scores remain average. Database operations and Redis SET operations both registered poor performance, reflecting the processing limitations of the dual-vCPU configuration under heavy concurrent loads.

The qualitative cost profile of the E8-2s_v5 is defined by its high memory density relative to its compute capacity. This configuration is cost-efficient for workloads that demand a large memory footprint but do not require intensive parallel processing. The primary tradeoff is the low compute-to-memory ratio, which makes it unsuitable for CPU-bound tasks like software compilation, ray tracing, or large-scale LLM inference. This server is best suited for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads such as small-scale caching layers, lightweight database hosting with high RAM requirements, or memory-bound data processing tasks that do not scale heavily across multiple threads.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-2s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv5 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 2 vCPUs from a single physical core and a substantial 64.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are poor due to the limited vCPU count. Consequently, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute tasks. It is best suited for workloads requiring large memory capacities with minimal parallel processing requirements, such as small database instances, caching layers, or memory-bound data processing.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density and dedicated Intel processing for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.
